---
title: Platform
---

Platform metrics for CanadaLogin, in the `sre` schema. Currently, both tables are
derived from the CanadaLogin Platform team's `CanadaLogin_MTTR_Dashboard` Google
Sheet (maintained by hand in the CDS Google Drive) and uploaded by hand by the
Data and Research team. They share one source and grain (one row per incident);
they differ only in history: `mttr_current` holds the latest state of each
incident, while `mttr_history` accumulates a dated snapshot of every incident on
each upload.

### Data quality

Because the source is a hand-maintained spreadsheet, a few caveats apply to both
tables:

- **Timestamps are strings, not native timestamps.** `detected_at`,
  `acknowledged_at`, `mitigated_at`, and `resolved_at` are text in America/Toronto
  local time at minute precision (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`). Parse them before doing date
  math. (`fetched_at` is a real timestamp, in UTC.)
- **URLs may carry trailing whitespace or embedded newlines.** Trim
  `slack_channel` and `postmortem_link` before joining on or rendering them.
- **Categorical columns are unvalidated free text.** `severity`, `service`,
  `component`, and `root_cause_category` are typed by hand, so expect occasional
  typos, casing, and spacing drift. The values listed in each field table are those
  observed so far, not a closed set.
- **The duration columns are rounded to the nearest minute** from second-precision
  source times, so recomputing them from the displayed (minute-precision) timestamp
  strings can differ by up to a minute.

{{< include sre/mttr_current.qmd >}}

{{< include sre/mttr_history.qmd >}}
